key_departures.impact: 11-year starter at RT retired due to mounting ankle/knee injuries after playing only 7 games in 2025. Warren McClendon Jr. (2023 5th-round pick) started 13 games in his absence and was publicly anointed as the starter by McVay. Departure is partially mitigated by McClendon's readiness, but creates a depth and long-term need at RT.

Veteran CB placed on Reserve/Retired list. Was still under contract at $7.5M cap hit, so retirement freed significant cap space. Was a declining starter — departure contributes to the pre-existing CB need but was anticipated.

Started 15 games in 2025 and was one of the Rams' more reliable corners. Inside-outside versatility. Departure leaves a hole in the starting CB rotation. Rams did not bring him back entering free agency.
Missed most of 2025 with clavicle injuries. Durability concerns made re-signing unlikely. Depth loss only.
Trade-deadline acquisition who played in the playoffs. Limited by hip injury. Exit creates minor depth loss but was never a locked-in starter.

Played only 16.5% of offensive snaps despite a fully guaranteed $10M contract in 2025. Departure expected and welcomed — opens a WR3 roster spot but the Rams still need a third receiver behind Nacua and Adams.

Backup QB hits open market. Reports linked him to Arizona (Cardinals hired former Rams OC Mike LaFleur as HC). Creates a need for a backup QB behind Stafford, but not a critical one given Stafford's return.
Backup OT who struggled in limited appearances. Depth loss only — Quessenberry re-signed fills the swing tackle role.

contract.notes: 4-year extension signed days after trade; $13.6M fifth-year option cap hit in 2026 before extension kicks in for 2027. Extension makes him NFL's highest-paid CB ever.

signings.analysis: Record-setting deal — $31M AAV and $100M guaranteed obliterate prior CB market records (Sauce Gardner at $30.1M, Stingley at $89M guaranteed). Two-time All-Pro, first-team All-Pro in 2023. Traded for 4 picks including the No. 29 overall selection, signaling full commitment. He is the unquestioned CB1 for the next 4+ years. Paired with Emmanuel Forbes and the Watson addition, CB goes from the team's single biggest need to a genuine strength.

contract.notes: Deal averages $17M/yr with $15.5M paid in 2026, $18.5M in 2027, $17M in 2028. Rams have a soft out after Year 1 but owe $11M if cut. Confirmed per Tom Pelissero/AP.

signings.analysis: Solid CB2 signing at mid-market AAV with real guarantees. Watson started 29 games in KC, had career-best 2025 season (64 tackles, 2 INTs, 6 PBUs). At $17M AAV, this is a legitimate starter-tier investment, not a bridge deal. Combined with McDuffie and returning Emmanuel Forbes, the Rams now have three quality outside corners. CB need is comprehensively resolved — goes from Tier 1 critical to at most Tier 4 depth.

contract.notes: Max value $39M. $5M signing bonus prorated over 3 years, $1.5M roster bonus. 2026 salary of $4.6M fully guaranteed. Effectively a 2-year deal per structure. Confirmed per CBS, SGV Tribune, OTC.

signings.analysis: Curl played 99% of defensive snaps in 2025, had career-high 122 tackles and 2 INTs including a playoff INT. Re-signing him at $12M AAV (mid-market for his level) locks in a quality starter. Combined with in-season extension for Quentin Lake, the Rams now have both starting safeties under multi-year deals. S need is fully resolved for 2026 and likely 2027.

contract.notes: $8M is max value per Adam Schefter/agent Erik Burkhardt. Guarantee details not reported — estimated low given age/injury history. At $4M AAV this is depth-tier money.

signings.analysis: At $4M AAV max, this is effectively a team-friendly depth signing for a 33-year-old who missed significant time in each of the last two seasons. TE was not a need — the Rams have Colby Parkinson, Davis Allen, and Terrance Ferguson all under contract. Higbee's value is leadership, blocking, and McVay's 13-personnel scheme continuity. Does not change need picture.

contract.notes: Reported by Arrowhead Pride/Ramblinfan at $3.45M total over 2 years. Replaces Jake McQuaide.

signings.analysis: Special teams was a documented weakness in 2025 — the Rams cycled through two long snappers mid-season. Cardona is a proven 11-year veteran with two Super Bowl rings (NE). At $1.725M AAV, this is fair market value for a quality LS. Addresses a position that directly contributed to playoff losses. LS need resolved.

contract.notes: Confirmed per therams.com and Yahoo Sports. Swing tackle role.

signings.analysis: One-year deal at $1.5M is a pure depth/swing tackle re-sign. Does not solve the long-term RT question post-Havenstein retirement. McClendon is the presumed starter at RT; Quessenberry competes for a roster spot as backup. OT remains a mid-tier need for the future.

contract.notes: ERFA tender at league minimum. Confirmed by therams.com.

signings.analysis: ERFA tender — cost-controlled depth. Can play both guard spots. Does not address any starting-caliber IOL need.

contract.notes: ERFA tender at league minimum. Confirmed by therams.com.

signings.analysis: Mevis took over as kicker mid-season and was consistent after replacing Joshua Karty. ERFA tender is the right move at minimum cost. Kicker is not a premium need but special teams overall remains a work in progress.

contract.notes: ERFA tender at league minimum. Confirmed by therams.com.

signings.analysis: ERFA tender. Had a muffed punt in 2025 but also made some offensive contributions. Does not address the WR3 need left by Tutu Atwell's departure. Camp body / ST contributor.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: McDuffie (4yr/$124M, $100M gtd, highest-paid CB in NFL history) + Watson (3yr/$51M, $34M gtd) completely overhaul the CB room. Add Emmanuel Forbes (returning starter) and the re-signed Quentin Lake (slot/nickel). CB was the biggest need entering the offseason; it is now a strength. At most, they draft developmental depth on Day 3.
Cam Curl re-signed (3yr/$36M, $24M gtd) locks in a starter who played 99% of snaps in 2025. Quentin Lake was extended mid-season. Both safeties are under multi-year deals. S need fully resolved.

Tutu Atwell departed (was essentially unusable at 16.5% snap rate), creating a genuine WR3 vacancy. Puka Nacua (WR1) and Davante Adams (WR2, age 33, restructure candidate) are set. Xavier Smith ERFA tender is camp depth, not a solution. The Rams need a legitimate WR3 contributor, either through free agency or the draft. Need escalated from depth to genuine.

Rob Havenstein retired after 11 seasons. Warren McClendon Jr. (2023 5th-rounder) is the likely starter at RT — McVay affirmed this publicly — but he has not proven himself over a full season. Quessenberry re-sign ($1.5M, 1-yr) is pure depth. The team still needs to identify a long-term RT starter and add reliable backup OT depth. LT Alaric Jackson is set. RT is now a Tier 2 need — prioritize in Rounds 2–4 of the draft.

Jimmy Garoppolo departed, leaving Matthew Stafford (38, reigning MVP, year-to-year arrangement) with Stetson Bennett as the only backup. Garoppolo was a capable backup who understood the system. The Rams need to add a veteran backup QB — not a starter replacement, but more than a camp arm. Need elevated from depth to real.
No meaningful changes at EDGE. Jared Verse (2024 1st-round pick) is entrenched as the starter. Josaiah Stewart showed promise. No departures at the position. Need unchanged.
No EDGE/IDL moves made in free agency. Kobie Turner, Braden Fiske, and Poona Ford form the base. No changes — need unchanged.
Steve Avila and Kevin Dotson are under contract at guard. Justin Dedich ERFA-tendered for depth. No upgrades at center or guard made in free agency. Interior OL depth remains a concern for the draft.

key_additions.headline: Record-breaking CB acquisition (trade + extension) makes him NFL's highest-paid corner and instantly transforms LA's biggest weakness into a strength
Former Chiefs teammate rejoins McDuffie, giving Rams a proven CB2 who just completed his best NFL season
Breakout safety re-signed after playing 99% of defensive snaps in 2025, anchoring a dramatically improved secondary
Franchise's all-time leading TE returns on a team-friendly deal to anchor McVay's three-TE offensive identity
Veteran two-time Super Bowl champion long snapper stabilizes a special teams unit that cost the Rams multiple games in 2025

key_losses.headline: 11-year franchise pillar and Super Bowl LVI champion retires, opening the RT starting job for Warren McClendon Jr.
Veteran CB retires, freeing $7.5M in cap space and removing a declining starter from a secondary the Rams have since fully rebuilt
15-game starter and versatile inside-outside corner departs in free agency after the Rams pivoted to McDuffie and Watson
Speed receiver departs after $10M contract yielded only 16.5% snap share, creating an open WR3 competition
System-savvy backup QB likely headed to Arizona (Cardinals hired ex-Rams OC LaFleur), leaving Rams needing a new Stafford understudy

notes: All transactions confirmed via NFL Network, ESPN, AP, and team sources. McDuffie extension ($100M guaranteed) confirmed Sunday March 9 per Schefter/Rapoport. Watson deal confirmed Monday March 10 per AP/Pelissero. Cardona deal confirmed Monday March 10 per ESPN's Mike Reiss. Legal tampering began March 9; new league year opens March 11. All deals become official March 11. Contract guarantee figures for Higbee not officially disclosed — estimated as minimal/zero given age (33) and injury history. OTC live cap space of ~$20.5M reflects current committed contracts, not full roster-build post-March 11.
